{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1374 restores fair treatment for all utility customers with on-site solar generation.",
    "The CPUC decision forces customers with multiple meters to sell self-generated solar energy at low rates and buy it back at higher rates.",
    "The inability to self-consume the energy generated by organizations is financially detrimental to classrooms and families facing high energy costs.",
    "SB 1374 requires the CPUC to update its tariffs to treat properties with multiple meters the same as single-family homes.",
    "The measure accelerates California's transition to a clean and reliable grid.",
    "California cannot meet its carbon emission reduction goals without properly supporting over 10,000 school sites in the transition to clean energy.",
    "Solar energy and battery storage are critical to protecting learning time in schools.",
    "The rising electricity costs in schools affect the overall funding available for educational programs.",
    "Restoring and protecting solar incentives is crucial for improving educational equity."
